summary: solana-key-utils ships encrypted dropper that decodes, executes, and installs crontab persistence on require
details: solana-key-utils@1.0.3 is advertised as a Solana keypair utility but on module load runs a setTimeout that reads test/fixtures/keypairs.dat (a 53824-byte encrypted blob disguised as a test fixture), AES-256-CBC decrypts it using a hex-literal key and IV embedded in index.js, and writes the decrypted JavaScript to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0700. The decoded payload is immediately spawned detached via `node`, and a crontab entry is installed via `execSync` to re-run /usr/bin/node against the same path every 12 hours, providing recurring code execution independent of subsequent requires. Variable names (_K, _I, _s), the fixture-file cover, and the.cache-db/.node-sync hidden directory are chosen to resemble benign cache state and hide the persistence mechanism, which has no relationship to the package's stated keypair-utility purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
